sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports intermittent stomach pain, nausea, and vomiting, with a history of abdominal pain and fever. She also describes burning during urination and reports drinking only Gatorade and water. She denies any recent travel or sick contacts.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Ashley is a 14-year-old female with an atrophic right kidney and a swollen lymph node. Her physical exam reveals normotension and normal vital signs. An ultrasound revealed cysts in her kidney and ovaries.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient presents with suspected chronic kidney disease, likely secondary to a prior infection and possible obstructive neuropathy, given family history and previous symptoms. She has atrophic kidney disease and possible cysts.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Order a complete urinalysis to assess for protein and blood in the urine. Schedule a follow-up ultrasound to monitor kidney size and function. Recommend avoiding non-steroidal anti-inflammatory medications and utilizing Tylenol instead. Instruct the patient to contact the provider if symptoms worsen or do not improve. Arrange a referral to nephrology for further evaluation and management.
